title: dactylorhiza majalis subsp. sesquipedalis
intro: Dactylorhiza majalis subsp. sesquipedalis, also known as the long-spurred marsh orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to Europe and western Asia, characterized by its distinctive long spur on the labellum. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza majalis subsp. sesquipedalis include its potential to aid in the treatment of urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and other kidney-related issues, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture or inflorescence, and it is also used in traditional medicine to make teas and infusions that are used to treat various health conditions.
